revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with '[[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|...'
revision.text:
[[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Lang-ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]<ref>[https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html Official Gazette Overview (National Printing Bureau)]</ref><ref>[https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) (The Japan Times)]</ref>. Its official publication started in 1886, from [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of Finance]].
Japan traditionally relied on the [[Kōsatsu]], a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places to let the public in general know the [[shogun]]'s nationwide or [[daimyo]]'s local proclamations<ref>[https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki)]</ref>. As the people's [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the Kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kanpo]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
[[Category:Category:Government gazettes]] [[Category:Newspapers published in Japan]] [[Category:Media in Japan‎]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Lang-ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]].<ref>[https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html Official Gazette Overview (National Printing Bureau)]</ref><ref>[https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) (The Japan Times)]</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>[https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki)]</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Lang-ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |archive-url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref> <ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>[https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki)]</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Lang-ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>[https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki)]</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}
[[Category:Newspapers established in 1886]] [[Category:1886 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Government gazettes]] [[Category:Newspapers published in Japan]] [[Category:Mass media in Japan]]

Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[National Printing Bureau]] of the [[Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[Meiji government]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[government gazette]], which is now available on the [[Internet]].
==See also== *[[Public notice]] *[[Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

[[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[:en:Kasumigaseki|Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[:en:Government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[:en:National Printing Bureau|National Printing Bureau]] of the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[:en:literacy rate|literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]], which is now available on the [[:en:Internet|Internet]].

|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[:en:National Printing Bureau|National Printing Bureau]] of the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[:en:literacy rate|literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]], which is now available on the [[:en:Internet|Internet]].
==See also== *[[:en:Public notice|Public notice]] *[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Official Gazette (Japan)]][[en:Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)]][[it:Bollettino ufficiale (Giappone)]][[ja:官報]][[ko:일본의 관보]][[zh:官报]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Official Gazette (Japan)]][[en:Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)]][[it:Bollettino ufficiale (Giappone)]][[ja:官報]][[ko:일본의 관보]][[zh:官报]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Official Gazette (Japan)]][[en:Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)]][[it:Bollettino ufficiale (Giappone)]][[ja:官報]][[ko:일본의 관보]][[zh:官报]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452096]] Wikidata name: Official Gazette [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452096]] Wikidata name: Official Gazette [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[:en:Kasumigaseki|Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[:en:Government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[:en:National Printing Bureau|National Printing Bureau]] of the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[:en:literacy rate|literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]], which is now available on the [[:en:Internet|Internet]].
==See also== *[[:en:Public notice|Public notice]] *[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[:en:National Printing Bureau|National Printing Bureau]] of the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Official Gazette (Japan)]][[en:Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)]][[it:Bollettino ufficiale (Giappone)]][[ja:官報]][[ko:일본의 관보]][[zh:官报]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Official Gazette (Japan)]][[en:Kanpō (Japanese government gazette)]][[it:Bollettino ufficiale (Giappone)]][[ja:官報]][[ko:일본의 관보]][[zh:官报]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452096]] Wikidata name: Official Gazette [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452096]] Wikidata name: Official Gazette [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]]]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at [[:en:Kasumigaseki|Kasumigaseki]], Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the [[:en:Government gazette|official gazette]] of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the [[:en:National Printing Bureau|National Printing Bureau]] of the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of Finance]].
Japanese people historically relied on the ''[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the [[:en:literacy rate|literacy rate]] improved and the modern nation emerged under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]], the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese [[:en:Government gazette|Government gazette]], which is now available on the [[:en:Internet|Internet]].
==See also== *[[:en:Public notice|Public notice]] *[[:en:Kōsatsu|Kōsatsu]]
==References== {{Reflist}}

Japanese people historically relied on the ''{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu}}'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the {{ill|literacy rate|en|literacy rate|WD=Q369992|lt=literacy rate}} improved and the modern nation emerged under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}}, the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette}}, which is now available on the {{ill|Internet|en|Internet|WD=Q75|lt=Internet}}.
==See also== *{{ill|Public notice|en|Public notice|WD=Q1004391|lt=Public notice}} *{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu}}

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette}}]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at {{ill|Kasumigaseki|en|Kasumigaseki|WD=Q1268102|lt=Kasumigaseki}}, Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=official gazette}} of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the {{ill|National Printing Bureau|en|National Printing Bureau|WD=Q130035|lt=National Printing Bureau}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|en|Ministry of the Treasury|WD=Q112323848|lt=Ministry of Finance}}.
Japanese people historically relied on the ''{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu}}'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the {{ill|literacy rate|en|literacy rate|WD=Q369992|lt=literacy rate}} improved and the modern nation emerged under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}}, the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette}}, which is now available on the {{ill|Internet|en|Internet|WD=Q75|lt=Internet}}.
==See also== *{{ill|Public notice|en|Public notice|WD=Q1004391|lt=Public notice}} *{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu}}

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette|1=Government gazette}}]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at {{ill|Kasumigaseki|en|Kasumigaseki|WD=Q1268102|lt=Kasumigaseki|1=Kasumigaseki}}, Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=official gazette|1=Government gazette}} of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the {{ill|National Printing Bureau|en|National Printing Bureau|WD=Q130035|lt=National Printing Bureau|1=National Printing Bureau}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|en|Ministry of the Treasury|WD=Q112323848|lt=Ministry of Finance|1=Ministry of the Treasury}}.
Japanese people historically relied on the ''{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu|1=Kōsatsu}}'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the {{ill|literacy rate|en|literacy rate|WD=Q369992|lt=literacy rate|1=Literacy rate}} improved and the modern nation emerged under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}}, the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|WD=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette|1=Government gazette}}, which is now available on the {{ill|Internet|en|Internet|WD=Q75|lt=Internet|1=Internet}}.
==See also== *{{ill|Public notice|en|Public notice|WD=Q1004391|lt=Public notice|1=Public notice}} *{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu|1=Kōsatsu}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}

==See also== *{{ill|Public notice|en|Public notice|WD=Q1004391|lt=Public notice|1=Public notice}} *{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|WD=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu|1=Kōsatsu}}

{{Short description|Government gazette of Japan}} [[File:Japan official Gazette logo.png|thumb|right|130px|The logo of the Kanpō, the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|qid=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette|1=Government gazette}}]] [[File:SeifuKankoubutsuSC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The former Government Publications Service Center at {{ill|Kasumigaseki|en|Kasumigaseki|qid=Q1268102|lt=Kasumigaseki|1=Kasumigaseki}}, Tokyo]] '''Kanpō''' ({{Langx|ja|官報}}, literally "Official report") is the {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|qid=Q2065227|lt=official gazette|1=Government gazette}} of the [[:en:Japanese government|Japanese government]]. <ref>{{cite web |title=Official Gazette Overview |url=https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |website=National Printing Bureau |access-date=March 31, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230505130546/https://www.npb.go.jp/en/books/kanpo.html |archive-date=May 5, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Colin |title=The Kanpo: Where everything in Japan goes to happen (officially) |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |access-date=March 31, 2024 |work=The Japan Times |date=August 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240303160717/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/community/2018/08/05/issues/kanpo-everything-japan-goes-happen-officially/#.XmAo06hKjb0 |archive-date=March 3, 2024}}</ref> Its official publication started in 1886, from the {{ill|National Printing Bureau|en|National Printing Bureau|qid=Q130035|lt=National Printing Bureau|1=National Printing Bureau}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|en|Ministry of the Treasury|qid=Q112323848|lt=Ministry of Finance|1=Ministry of the Treasury}}.
Japanese people historically relied on the ''{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|qid=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu|1=Kōsatsu}}'', a wooden plaque placed at [[shukuba]] and other important places, to learn of proclamations made by either the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} or the local [[daimyo]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |title=Kosatsu (SamuraiWiki) |access-date=2020-03-04 |archive-date=2015-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150909230302/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Kosatsu |url-status=dead }}</ref> As the {{ill|literacy rate|en|literacy rate|qid=Q369992|lt=literacy rate|1=Literacy rate}} improved and the modern nation emerged under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|qid=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}}, the kōsatsu was abolished in 1873 and eventually replaced by the ''Kanpō'', the Japanese {{ill|Government gazette|en|Government gazette|qid=Q2065227|lt=Government gazette|1=Government gazette}}, which is now available on the {{ill|Internet|en|Internet|qid=Q75|lt=Internet|1=Internet}}.
==See also== *{{ill|Public notice|en|Public notice|qid=Q1004391|lt=Public notice|1=Public notice}} *{{ill|Kōsatsu|en|Kōsatsu|qid=Q11670187|lt=Kōsatsu|1=Kōsatsu}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kanpou.npb.go.jp/ Kanpo Internet Kanpo (National Printing Bureau)] in Japanese *[https://kanpo.net/ Government Publications Service Centers of Kanpo Ltd.] in Japanese
{{Authority control}}
